name: Castillo de la Mota Loop
name_original: Castillo de la Mota — circular por Astudillo
description: This easy 4.1-mile circular hike around Astudillo features the historic Castillo de la Mota and unique underground dwellings.
region_intro: Hiking around Astudillo offers diverse outdoor experiences in a transitional landscape between the Cerrato Palentino and Tierra de Campos regions. The area features rolling Castilian plains, agricultural fields, and hills, with the Pisuerga River influencing local trails. Hikers can explore routes that traverse varied terrain, including areas near historical gypsum caves and the impressive Canal de Castilla. This geographical blend provides a range of natural features for exploration. Best hiking trails around Astudillo The most popular hiking route is Castillo de la Mota Loop, a 4.1 miles (6.5 km) trail that takes 1 hour 47 minutes to complete. This easy route features gentle elevation changes through the local landscape. Another top favourite among local hikers is Río Pisuerga Riverbank Loop, an easy 4.1 miles (6.5 km) path. This trail follows the banks of the Pisuerga River, offering flat and accessible terrain. Local hikers also love the Yeseras de Astudillo – Astudillo loop from Astudillo, a 6.0 miles (9.6 km) trail leading through varied terrain, including areas historically known for gypsum extraction, often completed in about 2 hours 35 minutes. Hiking around Astudillo is defined by rolling plains, agricultural fields, and riverbank paths, with options for exploring historical gypsum sites and the Canal de Castilla. The network offers routes suitable for both easy walks and more moderate excursions. The routes in Astudillo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 5 reviews. More than 30 hikers have used komoot to explore Astudillo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Astudillo?
A: There are over 6 hiking routes documented on komoot around Astudillo, offering a variety of experiences. These trails range from easy walks along riverbanks to more moderate excursions through varied terrain.

Q: What are the typical distances and durations for hikes in Astudillo?
A: Hikes around Astudillo generally range from 5 to 12 kilometers (3 to 7.5 miles) in length. Most routes can be completed within 1.5 to 3 hours, making them suitable for half-day excursions. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1070197620/castillo-de-la-mota-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de la Mota Loop&lt;/a&gt; is about 6.5 km and takes under 2 hours.

Q: Are there any easy, family-friendly hiking options in Astudillo?
A: Yes, Astudillo offers several easy routes perfect for families. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1070187408/rio-pisuerga-riverbank-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Pisuerga Riverbank Loop&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 6.5 km path that follows the river, offering flat and accessible terrain. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1067290423/boadilla-del-camino-circular-along-the-banks-of-the-canal-de-castilla" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Boadilla del Camino — circular along the banks of the Canal de Castilla&lt;/a&gt; is another gentle option, ideal for a relaxed outing.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Astudillo?
A: The region around Astudillo is enjoyable for hiking year-round, but spring and early summer are particularly beautiful. During these months, the agricultural fields display vibrant green and red hues, and wildflowers bloom along trails like the Sendero Ermita de Torre Marte. Autumn also offers pleasant conditions with milder temperatures and golden landscapes.

Q: Are there circular hiking routes available in Astudillo?
A: Yes, many of the trails around Astudillo are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1070197620/castillo-de-la-mota-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de la Mota Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38159468" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Yeseras de Astudillo – Astudillo loop from Astudillo&lt;/a&gt;, which explore different aspects of the local landscape.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Astudillo?
A: Most trails in Astudillo are suitable for dogs, but it's always recommended to keep them on a leash, especially when passing through agricultural fields or areas where local wildlife like hares, foxes, and various bird species might be present. This also helps protect the natural environment and livestock.

Q: What kind of wildlife can I expect to see while hiking in Astudillo?
A: The diverse landscapes around Astudillo, including agricultural fields, forests, and riverbanks, are home to various wildlife. Hikers often spot hares, foxes, and a variety of bird species such as kestrels, partridges, and larks. The oak and scrub forests in the Cerrato Palentino region also host numerous passerine birds.

Q: What interesting historical or natural landmarks can I see along the trails?
A: Astudillo's trails offer a blend of natural beauty and historical intrigue. You can explore historical gypsum caves along routes like Sendero Las Yeseras, which were vital for the town's economy. The impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/148691" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canal de Castilla&lt;/a&gt;, an 18th-century engineering feat, also provides scenic walking paths. Other notable points of interest include the Hermitage of Torre Marte and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1012824" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Quadruple Lock of the Canal de Castilla&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any routes that follow the Pisuerga River?
A: Yes, the Pisuerga River plays a significant role in the local landscape. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1070187408/rio-pisuerga-riverbank-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Pisuerga Riverbank Loop&lt;/a&gt; is an easy path that follows the river's banks. Additionally, the Ruta Bajada al Río (Route 4) descends through old vineyards to the Pisuerga River, where swimming is even possible during summer months.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Astudillo?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars. Reviewers often praise the diverse landscapes, from rolling plains and agricultural fields to riverbank paths and historical sites. The well-marked trails and the opportunity to explore both natural beauty and cultural heritage are frequently highlighted.

Q: What should I wear for hiking in Astudillo during summer?
A: During summer, Astudillo can experience warm temperatures. It's advisable to wear lightweight, breathable clothing, a hat, and sunglasses. Bring plenty of water, as some routes may have limited shade. Sturdy walking shoes are always recommended, regardless of the season, to navigate varied terrain.

Q: Are there any hikes that offer panoramic views of Astudillo?
A: Yes, the Sendero Las Yeseras route, which journeys through pine forests, culminates in a firebreak that offers panoramic views of Astudillo. This moderate 8.3 km trail provides excellent vantage points to appreciate the town and its surrounding landscapes.
tips: Beautiful little castle surrounded by underground mountain dwellings

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this trail?
faqs.answer: This trail is considered easy and is suitable for most fitness levels. It involves a relatively gentle ascent with a total elevation gain of approximately 108 meters.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Castillo de la Mota circular walk?
faqs.answer: The route follows 'sendas' (paths) around the area. The Castle of La Mota of Astudillo itself is situated on an artificial mound atop a gentle hill, providing a slight elevation. You'll encounter a blend of historical and natural scenery.

faqs.question: What historical landmarks can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The primary landmark is the Castle of La Mota of Astudillo, a 12th-13th century fortification. Although largely in ruins, it offers a glimpse into medieval history and panoramic views. You may also encounter 'underground mountain dwellings' (viviendas subterráneas de montaña).

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for families with children?
faqs.answer: Yes, given its easy difficulty and relatively short distance of about 6.5 kilometers, this circular route is generally suitable for families. The historical castle provides an interesting focal point.

faqs.question: Is the Castillo de la Mota — circular by Astudillo trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While specific regulations are not provided, it is generally advisable to keep dogs on a leash, especially around historical sites and in areas where wildlife may be present. Please ensure you clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas are not detailed, Astudillo is the starting point for this circular route. You should be able to find parking within or near the town, close to the access points for the paths leading to the castle.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this hike?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits or entrance fees required to hike this circular trail or to visit the ruins of the Castle of La Mota of Astudillo.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed year-round. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summer can be warm, so an early start is recommended. Winter hikes are possible, but be prepared for cooler temperatures and potentially muddy paths.

faqs.question: Are there any viewpoints along the Astudillo circular walk?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Castle of La Mota of Astudillo, situated on a rise, offers panoramic views over the flat expanse of the Palencia region.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route also pass through parts of the Paseo Alejandro Mieres and Calle Rampojo.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or restrooms near the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail starts and ends in Astudillo, a town where you can find local cafes, restaurants, and other amenities. It's advisable to use facilities in town before starting your hike as there are none directly on the trail.

waypoints.name: Castle of La Mota of Astudillo
